title: Swida × czerniaewii
taxonomy: Swida × czerniaewii is a hybrid species within the genus Swida, commonly known as elderberries. The classification of this plant falls under the family Adoxaceae, which is a group of flowering plants. Within the family Adoxaceae, Swida × czerniaewii is classified as a member of the order Dipsacales. This classification is based on morphological and molecular characteristics of the plant. The species is further classified as Swida × czerniaewii, a combination of its genus name and the specific epithet 'czerniaewii' to denote its hybrid origin.

It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system.
